name: ProjectDiscovery Nuclei / PDCP API
what_it_does: Fast open-source vulnerability scanner with a YAML-based template system plus cloud platform API (PDCP) for managing scans, templates, and results at scale — supporting 9000+ community templates for CVE detection, misconfigurations, and web security.
use_cases: Running automated vulnerability scans against targets using community or custom templates
Managing scan schedules and tracking results via PDCP cloud platform API
Integrating CVE detection into CI/CD pipelines with template-based scanning
Building security automation workflows that trigger Nuclei scans on new deployments
Aggregating vulnerability scan results across many targets for security operations
not_for: Commercial enterprise DAST without open-source tooling (use Veracode or Checkmarx)
Scanning without authorization — Nuclei is for authorized security testing only
Non-technical teams needing GUI-only security scanning
best_when: A security engineering agent needs fast, template-driven vulnerability scanning with 9000+ community templates and programmatic result management.
avoid_when: You need a managed DAST service without running your own scanner — use Invicti or Detectify.

auth.notes: PDCP API key for cloud platform management. CLI tool (nuclei) uses API key for template sync and result upload. Open-source CLI can run without cloud account.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Nuclei CLI: completely free open-source; PDCP cloud: free tier available
pricing.paid_tiers: PDCP paid plans for team features, advanced scanning, and higher limits
pricing.notes: Nuclei itself is MIT-licensed and free. PDCP (ProjectDiscovery Cloud Platform) adds cloud management with free and paid tiers. Strong open-source community with 9000+ templates.

performance.rate_limits: Scan rate configurable in CLI; PDCP API has rate limits per plan

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: CLI documentation extensive and well-maintained. PDCP REST API documented with OpenAPI reference. Result parsing from JSONL output well-documented. Template writing documentation extensive.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Scan results upload idempotent by scan ID. Template sync operations are idempotent. Reading scan results via API is always idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: CLI-first tool — PDCP API is secondary; most power accessible via CLI subprocess
Template false positive rate varies — community templates need validation before production
Rate limiting during scans must be configured carefully to avoid blocking IPs
Authorized use only — running without permission is illegal in most jurisdictions

security_score_components.security_notes: MIT open source — full audit possible. API key auth. TLS enforced for PDCP. Tool purpose-built for authorized security testing. No data stored beyond PDCP if self-running. Use only against authorized targets.
